creator: sunpoet@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Dave Cottlehuber from comment #1)

I cannot reproduce this failure.

% ktserver -port 10001 -ulog 0001-ulog -sid 1 -bgs 0001-bgs -bgsi 10 ':#bnum=10000000#ktopts=p'

2017-01-12T04:04:54.633291+08:00: [SYSTEM]: ================ [START]: pid=22680
2017-01-12T04:04:54.634037+08:00: [SYSTEM]: opening the update log: path=0001-ulog sid=1

2017-01-12T04:04:54.635656+08:00: [SYSTEM]: starting the server: expr=:10001

2017-01-12T04:04:54.635889+08:00: [SYSTEM]: listening server socket started: fd=3
2017-01-12T04:05:04.809499+08:00: [INFO]: snapshotting databases

2017-01-12T04:05:14.990483+08:00: [INFO]: snapshotting databases

...

2017-01-12T04:08:49.081593+08:00: [ERROR]: database error: 0: success: no error
^C
2017-01-12T04:08:55.969249+08:00: [SYSTEM]: server stopped

2017-01-12T04:08:55.970062+08:00: [SYSTEM]: closing the server socket
2017-01-12T04:08:56.480556+08:00: [SYSTEM]: snapshotting databases
2017-01-12T04:08:56.586767+08:00: [ERROR]: database error: 0: success: no error
2017-01-12T04:08:56.586834+08:00: [SYSTEM]: closing a database: path=:#bnum=10000000#ktopts=p
